feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is it possible to create and Exception with MC/1 for the sum of valuated stock of two warehouses?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to create an Exception in MC.1 with MC/1 Exception Creation, but I need to check as threshold value, the total SUM of two warehouses at the same time.

Let say I have one work center, and two warehouses. The point is to create an exception for the total value sum of the Valuated Stock for both warehouses taking into account the sum of the two warehouses at the same time, instead of each one separatedly.

Example:

 

Valuated stock at Warehouse A = 1000 EUR

Valuated stock at Warehouses B = 1500 EUR

Exception value = A+B > 2000 EUR.

 

Is that possible?

 

Regards.

entry.title: How to Handle VMI on Subcontracted Assemblies
entry.content:
Background:
We are currently transitioning to a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) model for a specific motor part that is used in two different higher-level assemblies (BOMs). One BOM will continue to use the motor we purchase directly from the manufacturer A, while the other BOM will use the same motor part number supplied directly to our subcontractor through a VMI program. Critical to note these motors are exactly the same and have the same material master.
Goal:
Our primary goal is to ensure accurate demand planning and avoid over-purchasing the motor we buy directly and send to subcontractor number 1. We need to make sure that the consumption of the VMI motor at the subcontractor number 2 is properly reflected in our SAP system so that it is considered when calculating the overall demand for the motor.
Challenges:

  • The VMI motor and the directly purchased motor share the same material master and part number, making it difficult to distinguish between them in SAP. That material master is used on two BOMs. One BOM is manufactured by subcontractor A and one is manufacturered by subcontractor B.
  • We need to find a way to automatically capture the VMI motor usage at the subcontractor and integrate it into our demand planning process.
    Potential Solutions:
    We are exploring the following solutions within SAP to achieve our goal:
  • Consumption-Based Planning (MRP): We want to leverage MRP to calculate the overall demand for the motor based on the consumption of both BOMs. This requires accurate recording of the VMI motor usage at the subcontractor (e.g., through goods issue transactions).
  • Subcontracting with Component Supplied by Vendor: We are investigating if this functionality can help us automatically track the VMI motor usage. This would involve creating a subcontracting purchase order where the vendor supplies the motor directly to the subcontractor, and the consumption is automatically recorded in SAP.
    Questions for the SAP Community:
  • Has anyone successfully implemented either of these solutions (MRP or Subcontracting) to track VMI motor usage and integrate it into demand planning?
  • Are there any specific configuration steps or best practices we should be aware of when setting up these processes in SAP?
  • Are there any alternative solutions or workarounds within SAP that we should consider?
  • Any insights or advice on potential challenges or pitfalls we might encounter during implementation would be greatly appreciated.
    We are open to any suggestions or recommendations from the SAP community on how to best achieve our goal of accurate demand planning and inventory management for this motor part.

entry.title: Embracing SAP Clean Core: A Strategic Imperative for Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving digital landscape, enterprises are constantly seeking ways to streamline operations, enhance agility, and drive innovation. SAP Clean Core emerges as a pivotal strategy in this quest, offering a path to a simplified, efficient, and future-ready enterprise core. This blog post explores the significance of SAP Clean Core, its alignment with SAP Activate methodology, the synergies with RISE with SAP, the transformative capabilities of S/4HANA, and the role of KTern.AI in facilitating this journey.

SAP Activate Methodology: The Blueprint for Success

The SAP Activate methodology is a flexible, agile framework designed to guide businesses through the implementation and deployment of SAP solutions. It encompasses best practices, guided configurations, and a clear methodology to ensure project success. By leveraging SAP Activate, organizations can accelerate their SAP S/4HANA implementations, ensuring a clean core that is devoid of unnecessary customizations and complexity.

RISE with SAP: Elevating Business Transformation

RISE with SAP is a comprehensive offering that simplifies the migration of ERP systems to the cloud. It encapsulates cloud solutions, infrastructure, and services, providing a seamless transition path. With RISE with SAP, businesses can benefit from embedded intelligence, real-time insights, and advanced technologies, all of which contribute to maintaining a clean core and fostering business resilience.

SAP S/4HANA: The Heart of Digital Transformation

At the core of the digital transformation journey lies SAP S/4HANA, SAP’s most advanced ERP suite. Designed to run on the in-memory SAP HANA database, SAP S/4HANA integrates functions from lines of business and industry solutions, offering unparalleled speed, simplicity, and efficiency. A clean core in SAP S/4HANA ensures that businesses can leverage real-time data processing and analytics, essential for informed decision-making and competitive advantage.

 

The Imperative of SAP Clean Core

A clean core is essential for businesses to adapt swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer expectations. It enables organizations to leverage the latest SAP product advancements and integrate new software releases promptly. Without a clean core, businesses face challenges such as increased operational costs, inflexibility, and technical debt, which can hinder innovation and growth.

 

 

Without a clean core, organizations may encounter several challenges:

Technical Challenges

  • Complex Upgrades: Customizations can complicate the upgrade process, leading to longer and more costly system updates
  • Reduced System Performance: A cluttered core with custom code can degrade system performance and reliability
  • Increased Technical Debt: Over time, customizations can accumulate, resulting in technical debt that hampers agility and innovation.
  • Integration Difficulties: Custom code can create dependencies that make it difficult to integrate with other systems or technologies.

Operational Challenges

  • Operational Instability: A non-standard core may lead to unpredictable system behavior and increased downtime
  • Compliance Risks: Deviations from standard configurations can pose risks to regulatory compliance and data integrity
  • Resource Intensiveness: Customized systems require more resources for maintenance and support

Strategic Challenges

  • Inhibiting Digital Transformation: A dirty core can hinder an organization’s ability to adapt to new business models and technologies.
  • Barriers to Innovation: Excessive customizations can limit the ability to leverage new SAP functionalities and innovations.
  • Change Management Difficulties: Transitioning to a clean core requires significant change management efforts, which can be challenging if the existing system is heavily customized.

In summary, not maintaining a clean core can lead to increased costs, reduced efficiency, and a lack of flexibility, which are detrimental to an organization’s digital transformation journey. It’s essential to adopt a strategic approach to ensure a clean core for long-term success and sustainability.

Organizations looking to clean up their SAP core can start by adopting a strategic approach that includes the following steps:

  1. Assessment of Customizations: Begin with a thorough assessment of the existing system to identify all customizations, modifications, and enhancement.
  2. Fit-to-Standard Analysis: Evaluate the necessity of each customization against the standard SAP S/4HANA capabilities. Retire any custom code that is no longer needed or can be replaced by standard functionality.
  3. Leverage SAP Best Practices: Adopt SAP best practices and guided configurations to align with standard processes.
  4. Use of SAP BTP for Extensions: Utilize SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to build extensions rather than modifying the core system.
  5. API-First Approach: Implement an API-first strategy to facilitate integration without altering the core system.
  6. Continuous Monitoring and Optimization: Establish a process for ongoing monitoring and optimization to ensure the core remains clean and aligned with business needs.
  7. Documentation and Governance: Maintain comprehensive documentation of all changes and establish strong governance to manage technical debt.

 

By following these steps, organizations can systematically clean up their SAP core, leading to a more streamlined, efficient, and agile system that is better equipped for the digital transformation journey.

 Accelerating the Journey to a Clean Core with AI powered Automation

KTern.AI stands out as a digital transformation suite that aids in achieving a clean SAP core It offers digital tools for assessment, governance, process orchestration, testing, and optimization. With KTern.AI, businesses can plan risk-free SAP transformations, ensure productive governance, orchestrate SAP process lifecycles, and avoid disruptions during releases.

In conclusion, maintaining a clean core is not just a technical necessity; it is a strategic imperative that underpins the digital transformation journey. By embracing SAP Activate methodology, RISE with SAP, and SAP S/4HANA, and leveraging tools like KTern.AI, businesses can navigate the complexities of digital transformation with confidence and clarity


For more reading: 

@https://blogs.sap.com/2023/07/01/clean-core-demystified-what-does-it-mean-and-how-to-achieve-it-with... 

@ https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud... 

.

entry.title: Creation of product master for retail system via Soap service is recommended?
entry.content:
Hello,

We working for one of our client on transformation from ECC to S4 Hana Private Cloud(On-premise)

Here, I need create Article Master or Product master (Retail System) via API's.

I have checked with the ODATA V2 and ODATA V4 API's and understood that we cannot use both the services for Retail Maintenance which is written in the respective API page.

Now i am looking for SOAP service and found the following API "Overview | Product Master - Replicate from Client to SAP S/4HANA | SAP Business Accelerator Hub". 

Please any let me know whether this SOAP service can be used for Retail System or not to create article master?

 

Regards,

Sunil

entry.title: Archive FI_ACCOUNT How to check the accounts that are available to Archive
entry.content:
Hi,

We need to archive object FI_ACCOUNT. We are aware that we need to mark to deletion Fi Account, but our question is. Is there some report that indicate what accounts can be archived? In other words what accounts can be archived but are not marked to deletion?

 

Best Regards

 

João

entry.title: Does an MRP run delete or cancel planned orders if the min/max are set to 0.
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

We are working on MM deletions. One of the checks is to ensure there are no orders, including planned orders (STPOs). Currently, there are STPOs for every material I want to delete.

The other conditions are as follows:

  1. No stock on hand of any kind, and
  2. No demand (reservations, work orders etc)

Is an MRP-run expected to or able to rid the system of the planned orders (STPOs)?

Thank you for your input.

Kind Regards,

PM

entry.title: Norwegian-registered foreign business
entry.content:
Hello!
We need to register businesses where the VAT-number is Norwegian, but the company is located in another country,

As an example: 
A swedish supplier has a Norwegian VAT-number, but a Swedish adress.

How do we solve this in ECC?

entry.title: SAP Business Partner address-independent communication
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
I want to find out the mailaddress from the address-independent communication in SAP Business Partner.
I already know, that the mailaddress can be found in table ADR6 together with a addressnumber.
But I didn't find a table, where those addressnumber is assigned to the nummber of the business partner.
In BUT020 or BUT021_FS there are no entrys for it.
Could you please tell me, in which table the address-independent addressnumber is assigned to the nummber of the business partner?
Regards
Gunnar Schenk

entry.title: cpu max on hd server
entry.content:
what is max cpu i can go upto in Database Server.

select * from M_HOST_INFORMATION where KEY = 'cpu_threads'

 

I have 128 cores now presently.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Reconciliation between asset subledger and general ledger
entry.content:
What report will allow me to reconcile previous months of posted depreciation from the asset subledger to the balance sheet accounts. The Asset History Sheet S_ALR_87011990 only provides numbers from the previously posted month. Thank you


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: link blue boxing name to work order sap
entry.content:
for our technicians we need to do some blueboxing where materials are combined in a box.

al those boxes have a number for example C203 or B201.

we would like to put the C203 somewhere in the order but there is nothing we can find that is also printed on the work orders and that we can see in the list we track in iw37N.

is there a field that we can use for this?

thanks in advance

entry.title: Bad sequence in luw from EWM to ERP
entry.content:
Hello,

I noticed a problem when integrating EWM queues into the ERP. it happens that the sequence of Luw is not the right one.

For example on inbound deliveries, several luws will fall into error "M7021 Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use" these luws correspond to the passage of stock from ROD to AFS.
I have to save them until the ROD reception's luw was integrated, then I restore the saved luws and everything is back to normal.

We cannot reproduce the problem, it appears approximately 5 times per month.

Is there a setting on EWM or ERP to fix this problem?

regards,

Nicolas ALBERT

entry.title: Depleting Inventory - notification to charge customer
entry.content:
Hello - 

There are a few products that we order that are "customer specific", which means that the raw material only goes into 1 or 2 finished goods, for 1 customer.  

For those instances, we have a supply agreement in place with the customer, saying that if the product expires, or they discontinue using that item, that they would be responsible for the balance of inventory.  

Is there a way in SAP, (not in an attachment in MM03) - that when the item is up for expiration, or when the item will be discontinued, a pop up comes up, referencing something like: "customer specific material, charge customer".

entry.title: Creating BOM with new Usage instead of Usage 'S' within Table STZU
entry.content:
When creating a Task List via IA05, a Material is added to an Operation which creates a BOM in the background.
When you check Table STZU, the 'Usage' is maintained as '4' (Plant Maintenance).
How-To configure this to be another (new) Usage-Type  instead of '4' (Plant Maintenance)?

It has BOM-Type S, can it be linked with this Type?

entry.title: Process order number range skipping from 40010 to 40020 and after few orders again its taking 40013
entry.content:
Hi

We have a buffer on process order number ranges as it is recommended by default. But while creating orders , for e.g it created 40010 then it would take 40020 but after few orders it again goes back and takes 40013. We want to avoid this but also do not want to impact the performance of the system considering the number of users.

please recommend a solution!

entry.title: Usage Decision QA11 (321 movement type)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

user done usage decision for 280pcs with 321 movmt type from quality to unrestricted, but  the stock is present in Q (quality control stock) in WM. i want the stock in Available stock.
can anyone please guide me how to do it?

thank you in advance

entry.title: In F110, the system select invoice for vendor that doesn't have the payment method in its MD. why ?
entry.content:
How is it possible to select only invoice from vendor having the selected payment method ?

 

Thank you

entry.title: About MF60 use?
entry.content: It has 2 warehouses. 1. Material warehouse --> The warehouse location where stocks are entered and stored. 2-Production warehouse --> Materials needed during production are transferred to this warehouse and planned orders are created in this warehouse location. Before production, we check the material readiness using the MF60 screen. This screen shows only the stocks in the production warehouse as available stock and gives a quantity deficit. In fact, it should show the quantity deficit using the total stock in production and material warehouse. Is there a method for this?

entry.title: How to make Foreign Trade: Export data tab visible in the material master
entry.content: How to make Foreign Trade: Export data tab visible in the material master?

entry.title: Skip assembly scrap calculation in production order
entry.content:
Hi all

In our company, we have products (rolls) that can be produced in 2 different ways:

  • The first way is via a new production run. In that case, assembly scrap needs to be applied.
  • Alternatively, the roll is produced by cutting a part of an existing roll. A separate production version exists for this. However, in that case, no assembly scrap needs to be taken into account.

As the first way of production is the default one, we entered assembly scrap in the material master. Now, we are looking for a solution to skip the assembly scrap for the 2nd way of production. Any idea how we can achieve this?

Regards

Joren
